subtaskTitle: Learn the definition and examples of gharar in contracts

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) forbade the sale of gharar (Muslim).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih Muslim 3614 (Book 10), which states that the Prophet forbade transactions involving uncertainty (gharar).

subtaskTitle: Identify and close any gaps — adjust spending, increase income, or seek community support

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih Muslim 2182 (Book 5): 'This sin is enough for a man that he withholds the subsistence from one whose master he is.'

subtaskTitle: Open an account and make your first investment, even if small

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said the most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small (Sahih al-Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Volume 1, Book 2, Number 41: 'the best deed (act of Worship) in the sight of Allah is that which is done regularly.' The exact phrasing 'even if small' appears in the parallel narration in Sahih Muslim 1711.

subtaskTitle: Learn what categories of property cannot be privately owned (e.g., public water sources)

subtaskTitle: Formalise ownership through proper documentation or return the asset if it is not yours

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever has wronged his brother, let him seek his pardon today before there will be no dinar or dirham" (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Volume 3, Book 43, Number 629. It instructs anyone who has wronged another to 'beg him to forgive him before the Day of Resurrection when there will be no money' (dinar or dirham).
